growth_records.strain: Mixed community; denitrification-dominant (fermenters and DNRA outcompeted at low Lac/N)
growth_records.medium: Mineral medium with DL-lactate + nitrate, Lac/N 0.63 (dual electron-donor/acceptor limitation)

yields.product_yields: all nitrate denitrified to N2 (apart from assimilation); fermentation and DNRA absent

growth_records.cite: van den Berg EM et al. Fermentative Bacteria Influence the Competition between Denitrifiers and DNRA Bacteria. Frontiers in Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2017.01684. [PMC5591879]
growth_records.snippet: Table 5A ... Lac/N 0.63: Lac -0.37+/-0.06, NO3- -0.59+/-0.02, H+ n.d., Biomass n.d., Ac- 0, Prop- 0, NH4+ 0, CO2 n.d. At this steady state, all influent nitrate was denitrified... effluent contained no residual lactate or fermentation produ
growth_records.notes: MIXED enrichment; denitrification-only steady state. Biomass, H+ and CO2 not determined so specific rates cannot be derived. Growth rate=D=0.027 h-1. Only lactate (-0.37) and nitrate (-0.59) net uptake reported (mmol/h).
